content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

December 23, 2005


Marie Michel Macias, City Clerk
City of Pomona
City Hall, 505 S. Garey Avenue
Pomona, CA 91769-3186

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-05-250

Dear Ms. Macias:

This letter is in response to your request on behalf of the City of Pomona for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

May the semi-annual statement for the second half of 2005 be combined with the semi-annual statement for the first half of 2006 in connection with the January 10, 2006, special election?

CONCLUSION

Candidates and committees involved in the January 10, 2006, special election may file a combined statement that will satisfy the requirements to file the semi-annual statement for the second half of 2005 and the semi-annual statement for the first half of 2006. The combined statement will be filed on July 31, 2006, covering the period December 25, 2005, through June 30, 2006. A filing schedule containing the filing deadlines and periods covered is enclosed with this letter.

FACTS

The City of Pomona is conducting a special election on January 10, 2006, to fill the mayoral vacancy.

¹ Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

Our File No. A-05-250
Page 2

ANALYSIS

During an election, candidates and committees involved in the election are
required to file two pre-election campaign statements disclosing contributions received
and expenditures made in connection with an election. These pre-election statements are
generally filed 40 days before the election and again 12 days before the election.
(Section 84200.8.) Semi-annual statements are filed January 31 and July 31,
respectively. (Section 84200.)

There is a mechanism in the Act that permits the combining of statements when
an overlapping reporting period occurs or the filing of a semi-annual statement would be
an additional, unnecessary filing. (Section 84205.) Furthermore, section 82018(b)
provides that the cumulative amount of contributions received and expenditures made in
one year in connection with an election to be held in another year may be calculated
ending on the closing date of the first semi-annual statement filed after the election. In
your situation, the closing date for the second pre-election statement is December 24,
2005, which leaves only seven days for the semi-annual reporting period ending
December 31, 2005. Therefore, we conclude that candidates and committees involved in
the January 10, 2006, special election may file a combined semi-annual statement, due
July 31, 2006, covering the period December 25, 2005, through June 30, 2006.

If I may be of any further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916)
323-6935.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Dixie Howard
Manager, Filing Officer Programs

Enclosure
